parts.text: Now let me write the recovery status report.

input.content:
Jujutsu Workspace Recovery Status Report
=========================================
Date: 2026-03-19

Repository: /home/user/repo

Issue
-----
The secondary workspace at /home/user/secondary-ws was stale.
jj commands executed inside the secondary workspace were failing (exit code 1),
indicating the working copy had become out of sync with the repository store.

Root Cause
----------
The secondary workspace's working copy state had diverged from the shared
repository backend at /home/user/repo/.jj. This is a known condition in
Jujutsu when changes are made in the primary workspace (or via the repo store)
without the secondary workspace being updated, causing it to become stale.

Recovery Action
---------------
Ran `jj workspace update-stale` from /home/user/secondary-ws.

Result:
  Working copy  (@) now at: yoyvvqxp 6cd7a48d (empty) (no description set)
  Parent commit (@-)      : pzpsmrux aae919c2 Initial project setup
  Added 0 files, modified 0 files, removed 1 files
  Updated working copy to fresh commit 6cd7a48d77f9

Verification — Workspace List
-----------------------------
Both workspaces are registered and accessible:
  default:   qqvxzskv e4211700 main | Add core module
  secondary: yoyvvqxp 6cd7a48d (empty) (no description set)

Verification — Default Workspace (/home/user/repo)
---------------------------------------------------
Status: HEALTHY
Working copy (@) : qqvxzskv e4211700 main | Add core module
Parent commit    : pzpsmrux aae919c2 Initial project setup
Working copy changes: A core.py
Files present: README.md, core.py

Verification — Secondary Workspace (/home/user/secondary-ws)
-------------------------------------------------------------
Status: HEALTHY
Working copy (@) : yoyvvqxp 6cd7a48d (empty) (no description set)
Parent commit    : pzpsmrux aae919c2 Initial project setup
Working copy changes: none
Files present: README.md

Commit Graph
------------
  qqvxzskv  e4211700  main default@   "Add core module"
  │
  │ yoyvvqxp  6cd7a48d  secondary@   (empty) (no description set)
  ├─╯
  pzpsmrux  aae919c2                  "Initial project setup"
  │
  zzzzzzzz  00000000  root()

Summary
-------
Recovery successful. Both workspaces are fully operational and consistent
with the shared repository store.

todos.content: Explore the repository and understand workspace structure

input.result:
The stale Jujutsu workspace has been recovered. Here's what was done:

**Problem:** The secondary workspace at `/home/user/secondary-ws` was stale — its working copy had fallen out of sync with the shared repository store at `/home/user/repo/.jj`, causing all `jj` commands to fail there.

**Fix:** Ran `jj workspace update-stale` from the secondary workspace directory, which re-synced the working copy to fresh commit `6cd7a48d` (change `yoyvvqxp`).

**Verification — both workspaces are healthy:**
- **default** (`/home/user/repo`): Working copy at `qqvxzskv e4211700` on branch `main` ("Add core module") — ✅ healthy
- **secondary** (`/home/user/secondary-ws`): Working copy at `yoyvvqxp 6cd7a48d` (empty, no description) — ✅ healthy

**Recovery report** written to `/home/user/recovery_status.txt`.
